title: IETF Internet-Draft: Bolyra Core Protocol Specification

artifacts.description: IETF Internet-Draft in RFC 7322 plain-text format covering all normative sections
RFC XML v3 (RFC 7991) source for the Internet-Draft, suitable for xml2rfc toolchain
CDDL schema for the CBOR-encoded proof payload wire format (IdentityProof, DelegationProof, NullifierProof)
CDDL schema describing public input/output signal arrays for each of the three Circom circuits
ABI specification (JSON) for the on-chain IBolyraVerifier interface, including verifyIdentity, verifyDelegation, and verifyNullifier entry points
Normative glossary: human, agent, delegator, delegatee, scope, nullifier, Merkle root, commitment — used as source-of-truth across all downstream documents
Shell script invoking cddl CLI to validate example CBOR payloads against the CDDL schemas
Binary CBOR example of a valid IdentityProof payload for test and spec illustration
Binary CBOR example of a valid DelegationProof payload
Binary CBOR example of a valid NullifierProof payload

steps: Establish normative terminology: define human, agent, delegator, delegatee, scope, nullifier, Merkle root, commitment in terminology.md; annotate each with RFC 2119/8174 usage level

Draft Protocol Overview section: describe the 3-circuit architecture (IdentityCircuit, DelegationCircuit, NullifierCircuit), their trust model, and the flow from off-chain proof generation to on-chain verification

Specify Circuit Public Interface: document public input signal schema (signal name, bit-width, encoding, semantic) and public output signal schema for each of the three Circom circuits in both prose and CDDL (cddl/circuit-io.cddl)

Define Wire Format section: specify CBOR encoding of proof payloads (Groth16 pi_a/pi_b/pi_c arrays, publicSignals, proofType enum, version), author cddl/proof-payload.cddl with CDDL notation, include annotated hex dumps as figures

Specify On-Chain Verification Interface: define IBolyraVerifier ABI (function selectors, calldata layout, return values, event signatures), encode as abi/IBolyraVerifier.json, reference EIP-712 structured-data hashing where applicable

Author Security Considerations section: replay attacks (nullifier binding, chain-id inclusion), Sybil resistance (commitment uniqueness guarantees), scope brute-force (entropy requirements and scope hashing scheme), root staleness (maximum root age parameter and on-chain root history depth)
Write Introduction section: motivation, target audience (CU identity, portable credentials), relationship to W3C DID/VC, non-goals

Assemble RFC XML v3 source (draft-bolyra-identity-core-00.xml) using xml2rfc boilerplate, IANA considerations (none for -00), references (RFC 2119, RFC 8174, RFC 8949 CBOR, RFC 8610 CDDL, Groth16 paper)
Run xml2rfc toolchain to produce draft-bolyra-identity-core-00.txt; fix any formatting errors reported by the tool
Create example CBOR payloads (tests/example-payloads/*.cbor) and validate them against cddl/proof-payload.cddl using tests/validate-cddl.sh
Cross-check all RFC 2119 keyword usages for consistency (no MUST in informative sections, no ambiguous SHOULDs without rationale)
Tag artifact versions and update the seed registry with artifact paths and section cross-references
dependencies: Circom circuit source files (for public signal enumeration)
cddl CLI or equivalent (for schema validation)
